Description: The 2008 global financial crisis was the most severe economic downturn since the Great Depression triggered primarily by a massive housing bubble collapse and subprime mortgage defaults. As high-risk loans lost value major institutions like Lehman Brothers collapsed causing credit markets to freeze and stocks to plummet. This fallout led to a worldwide recession with millions of job losses and unprecedented government bailouts to stabilize the system.

This crisis began in late 2009 with revelations of severe Greek debt irregularities, eventually spreading to Ireland, Portugal, Spain, and Italy. It raised widespread fears of a Eurozone collapse, resulting in massive international bailouts and strict austerity measures that caused significant social and economic hardship. The peak of the crisis lasted until July 2012, when the European Central Bank pledged to do whatever it takes to preserve the euro.

The COVID-19 pandemic was a transformative health crisis caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus leading to global disruptions and millions of deaths. Governments implemented lockdowns and travel restrictions that fundamentally altered the global economy and education systems. The crisis also spurred a historic scientific response resulting in rapid vaccine development and a permanent shift toward remote work.

The full-scale invasion of Russia on Ukraine in Feb 2022, caused an immediate and dramatic shock to global financial markets. The invasion led to sharp falls in stock prices worldwide and pushed oil prices above 100 dollars [er barrel for the first time in years. International sanctions and energy supply fears fueled intense risk aversion as the world assessed the long-term economic decoupling between the East and the West.

TheĀ2025 Spring Trade ShockĀrefers to the 40-day period of intense market volatility and diplomatic tension following the Liberation Day tariff proclamation in April. This window saw the rapid implementation of universal import taxes and aggressive reciprocal duties, which were largely paused or rolled back by mid-May after a significant global economic downturn.
